title: Nevada
excerpt:
Consumer Assistance in Nevada

The Nevada Consumer Assistance Program is run by the Nevada Governor’s Office for Consumer Health Assistance (GovCHA) in partnership with four non-profit groups: Access to Health Care Network (AHN), Rebuilding All Goals Efficiently (RAGE), State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP), and Salud en Accion (SEA). Staff can answer questions about health insurance regulations, rights, and responsibilities. They can help you file a complaint or appeal a health plan decision. Get consumer help with a problem or question.

Medicaid in Nevada

You can apply for Medicaid in Nevada by contacting your state Medicaid agency.

SHOP Health Insurance Marketplace® in Nevada

If your small business is located in Nevada, you can use the Nevada Health Link website to learn more about offering your employees health coverage.

SHOP Health Insurance Marketplace® for Employees in Nevada

If your employer offers SHOP health coverage in Nevada, you should contact your employer or visit the Nevada Health Link website for more information.

Health Insurance Marketplace® in Nevada

If you live in Nevada, you’ll use the Nevada Health Link website to enroll in health coverage.

CHIP in Nevada

Nevada Check Up covers uninsured children in families with moderate incomes that are too high to qualify for Medicaid. You can find out whether your children qualify for Nevada Check Up right now. Fill out a Marketplace application and find out whether your children qualify. Learn more about Nevada Check Up.
